model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 fixed training steps on full CIFAR-10, this MLP is likely undertrained at the current conservative Adam LR. Doubling LR is a simple, low-risk way to improve early optimization and reduce validation loss within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The higher LR degraded validation loss, so revert to the safer LR and test AdamW for better early-step regularized optimization under the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size lets the model see substantially more training examples within the same 256 updates, which is often beneficial for this undertrained MLP regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strong gain from 64 to 128 suggests this fixed-step run is data-exposure limited. Increasing to 256 should further increase unique examples seen within 256 updates and can reduce validation loss if optimization remains stable.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After moving to a much larger batch, a modest LR increase should better match gradient scale and improve progress within the fixed 256-step budget without the instability seen at 1e-3 earlier.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The LR increase hurt, so revert to 5e-4 and continue exploiting the strongest signal so far: larger batch under fixed-step evaluation to increase data exposure per update.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After large gains from increased data exposure, the next likely bottleneck is model capacity. Increasing depth from 2 to 3 adds representational power with moderate complexity and should still fit the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With larger batch and deeper network already helping, increasing base width is a direct capacity upgrade likely to improve fit on CIFAR-10 within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Cosine decay over the short budget hurt results, so disable it and continue along the strongest trend by increasing batch size further to maximize data exposure per fixed update.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After substantial gains from higher effective data throughput, the model may now be slightly over-regularized. Reducing weight decay can improve fit and lower validation loss in this short-budget regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The previous reduction in weight decay gave a slight improvement, so continue in the same direction to test whether further relaxing regularization improves short-budget fit.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At very large batch size, a slightly higher LR can improve optimization speed within fixed steps; this tests a moderate increase while staying below the previously unstable 7e-4 setting.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Revert the LR bump that regressed, then test a deeper MLP to increase representational power now that high-throughput batching has improved optimization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 4","new":"DEPTH = 5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Depth increases have consistently improved loss so far; testing one additional hidden layer is a direct continuation of the strongest architecture trend.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 5","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Depth 5 regressed and increased runtime, so revert to depth 4 and test a larger batch to further increase data coverage per fixed update while keeping the stronger architecture.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increasing batch size has been the most reliable improvement direction in fixed-step mode; raising to 2048 should further increase sample exposure per update.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With very large-batch updates, a slightly lower LR can stabilize optimization and improve final validation loss within the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With improved optimization at lower LR, a small additional reduction in weight decay may further reduce underfitting and improve validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2560","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling has delivered the largest consistent gains under fixed-step evaluation, and the current configuration is stable, so a final increase tests whether additional per-step data exposure yields another loss reduction.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
